title: astragalus wingatanus var. dodgeanus
side_effects: Astragalus wingatanus var. dodgeanus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and stomach pain, due to its high saponin content. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant can lead to allergic reactions, such as hives and difficulty breathing, in individuals with sensitivities. Long-term misuse of the plant has been linked to kidney damage and an increased risk of bleeding disorders. Furthermore, pregnant women should avoid using the plant altogether, as it may stimulate uterine contractions and exacerbate pre-existing conditions.
